source_alignment.subject: English
source_alignment.source_statement: Read most words quickly and accurately without overt sounding and blending when they have been frequently encountered, and reread books to build up fluency and confidence in word reading.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches rereading as confidence-building, with audio modelling and no public speed pressure.

objective.child_goal: I can reread a short text so it sounds smoother.
objective.statement: Use supported rereading to improve accuracy, expression and confidence with familiar words and short sentences.
misconceptions: Thinks rereading means the first read was a failure.
Reads quickly but skips words or punctuation.
Sounds out every familiar word instead of recognising it on reread.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Reading that sounds smooth and makes sense.
vocabulary.audio_script: Fluency means reading smoothly, accurately and with meaning.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Read again to make it smoother or clearer.
vocabulary.audio_script: Rereading helps your reading grow smoother.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A replay loop around a sentence.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Listen to the sentence and follow the words.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model fluent reading before learner practice.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Listen first. Then we will read together.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Words highlight in time with narration.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: First read helps us learn the path. Reread makes it smoother.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Rereading is a strategy strong readers use.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A bumpy sentence path becomes smoother on replay.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Notice how the question mark changes the voice.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: A question mark helps our voice sound like a question.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Question mark lifts at the end of a spoken sentence.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Read this sentence in two smooth phrase chunks.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise phrasing instead of word-by-word reading.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Sentence splits into two phrase tiles.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Pip why rereading is a strong-reader move.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I thought rereading meant I did badly. Is that true?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion compares first read and smoother reread badges.

manipulatives.purpose: Support rereading with model audio, word tracking, phrase chunks and confidence reflection.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners listen, follow highlighted words, reread phrase chunks and reflect on smoothness/accuracy.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: No public timed score; supports audio replay, dyslexia-friendly spacing and low-sensory highlighting.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave familiar word reread, phrase chunking and punctuation expression.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Follow modelled fluent reading with word highlighting.
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Audio quality is critical for KS1.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Group words into meaningful phrase chunks.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: sentence + phrase split choices + audio replay

variant_blueprints.purpose: Check missed or substituted words without speed pressure.
Reframe rereading as a strategy and collect confidence signal.
Maintain phrase reading and familiar word fluency.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue + familiar sentence + phrase decision

body.prompt: Which punctuation tells your voice this is a question?
body.text: Can we go to the park?

hints: A question mark helps the voice ask.
question_variants.explanation: The question mark shows the sentence is a question.

body.prompt: Pip read 'The frog jumped log.' Which word was skipped?
body.target_text: The frog jumped over the log.

hints: The frog jumped over the log.

choices: Because first reads are always bad

hints: It can make the sentence sound smoother.
question_variants.explanation: Strong readers reread to make reading smoother, clearer and more confident.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks rereading means the first read was a failure.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects negative confidence response or avoids reread prompt.
repair_sequence: Show first read and reread as two normal practice steps.
Ask learner to choose a positive reread reason.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner identifies rereading as a helpful strategy.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Reads quickly but skips words or punctuation.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner misses words in listen-read or chooses speed over accuracy.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner identifies skipped word or punctuation in a new sentence.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use gentle word highlighting and no racing effects.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static phrase blocks instead of moving highlights.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Model every sentence with high-quality narration and replay.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use larger spacing, line focus and phrase chunks.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow pause, replay and self-paced reread.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Avoid speed scores and frame reread as expert practice.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach reread, smooth, sentence, word, phrase and question.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate reread with phrase support; secure requires smoother reread, punctuation awareness and confidence over time.
evidence.parent_summary: Can reread short familiar texts to improve smoothness, accuracy and confidence.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Uses model audio, phrase chunks and rereading to improve fluency without speed pressure.
next_actions: Add oral recording review only with safeguarding controls.

qa.notes: Sample Year 2 fluency pack with confidence-safe rereading.
